Architectural Styles

Search and filter by era, region or building type to find the right architectural language for your project. Exterior design only.

1920s – 1970s

Modern

Clean lines, functional forms, no ornament.

Europe → GlobalRational
2000s – present

Contemporary

Current trends, evolving — mixed materials, clean geometry.

GlobalCurrent
1980s – present

Minimalism

Simplicity — 'less is more'.

GlobalPure
1950s – present

Scandinavian

Simplicity, functionality, nature.

Nordic EuropeSimple
1990s – present

Japanese Minimalism

Harmony, simplicity, nature.

JapanHarmonious
1980s – present

Mediterranean Contemporary

Warm, relaxed, timeless — stucco walls and clean forms.

Mediterranean · GlobalWarm
1990s – present

Tropical Modern

Indoor–outdoor living, climate responsive.

Tropics · GlobalOpen
1940s – 1970s

Desert Modernism

Mid-century modernism shaped by sun, heat and the desert.

USA SouthwestWarm
1900s – present

Organic Architecture

Building and landscape as one continuous whole.

USA · GlobalNatural
2010s – present

Biophilic

Architecture woven with living nature.

GlobalGreen
1990s – present

Industrial

Raw, urban, utilitarian — exposed structure, big windows.

GlobalRaw
1970s – present

High-Tech

The structure and services are the architecture.

UK · GlobalEngineered
2000s – present

Parametric

Fluid, futuristic, innovative — curves and complex geometry.

GlobalFluid
1980s – present

Deconstructivism

Experimental, dynamic, bold — fragmented, non-linear form.

GlobalExperimental
1950s – 1980s

Brutalism

Bold, massive, sculptural — exposed raw concrete.

Europe → GlobalBold
1945 – 1970

Mid-Century Modern

Clean lines, indoor–outdoor connection.

USA · GlobalOpen
1900 – 1920

Prairie Style

Long low horizontals married to the open prairie.

USA MidwestHorizontal
1750s – 1900s (revived since)

Neo-Classical

Traditional, formal, symmetrical — columns and proportion.

Europe → GlobalFormal
Antiquity (8th c. BCE – 4th c. CE)

Classical

The original orders — column, pediment and proportion.

Greece · RomeOrdered
15th – 16th century

Renaissance

The rebirth of classical order and human proportion.

Italy → EuropeHarmonious
17th – 18th century

Baroque

Movement, drama and grandeur in stone.

Italy → EuropeDramatic
1920s – 1940s

Art Deco

Glamorous, geometric, decorative — strong lines and symmetry.

France → USA → GlobalGlamorous
1890 – 1910

Art Nouveau

Nature flowing into line, curve and ornament.

EuropeOrganic
1850 – 1920

Beaux-Arts

Monumental classical grandeur, richly composed.

France → USAGrand
1837 – 1901

Victorian

Exuberant eclectic ornament and picturesque silhouettes.

Britain → GlobalEclectic
1740 – 1900

Gothic Revival

Soaring pointed arches and medieval romance reborn.

Britain → GlobalVertical
1840 – 1900

Romanesque Revival

Heavy stone, round arches and robust solidity.

USA · EuropeMassive
1880 – 1955

Colonial Revival

Refined symmetry recalling the colonial Georgian house.

USASymmetrical
Timeless / pre-modern

Vernacular

Built by tradition from local materials and climate.

Global (place-specific)Local
2000s – present

Sustainable / Passive

Eco-friendly, efficient, harmonious — green roofs and living façades.

GlobalEco
No styles match
